reviews.review_msg: I'm still trying to figure out how she marks things. It's totally arbitrary. The amount of work for this class was a bit ridiculous for a third year class, especially since we got the marking criteria for one assignment the day it was due. Plays favourites (2/68 students get full participation marks? Come on). Not horrible, but not outstanding.

I only read one of the assigned books, as most assignments (including the term paper) - aside from a critical review on a reading of your choice - have NOTHING to do with the course. They are totally supplementary. She's super picky about the mechanics of writing so she's a hard marker. Aside from that I thoroughly enjoyed the course.

She's a nice person but I didn't find this class helpful at all. Lectures frequently ran off topic and the reading list seemed totally supplementary. I didn't even bother to read the last 3. Her marking scheme is useless as well. She offers only grammatical corrections and no real feedback to help you with on your paper. I'll be avoiding her.

She knows her stuff. Lectures are engaging. However, kind of condescending and rude. She has her 'favorites' in the class. Questionable marking scheme. No final exam for the course, though, and for me that's a plus. I'm on the fence with her, but I wouldn't discourage you from taking the course because you will learn a lot.

Great prof. Comes across as a bit of dragon lady at first, but this is a ruse... she's a sweetheart. Go to her office hours and chat with her, find a paper topic that really interests you, and then run with it. She's perpetually disorganized, and can't figure out the AV stuff for love or money, but she's fantastic. Really first rate.

Karyn is a spectacular professor! She runs an interesting class with ample time for discussion. Yes, she gives writing assessments and flexible due dates. Firstly, its 3rd year, you should be able to do them no sweat. Secondly, she assigns them to HELP you do better on your Term paper. She gives ample opportunity to get top marks. Excellent prof!

I learned so much from this class--this is one of the few profs who has helpful, useful feedback on improvement. My writing is much more coherent and correct because of this class.  Great discussions, great textual information, wonderful flexibility with interests and assignments. Yes, she doesn't baby you through the course--you're in 3rd year.
I found this prof to be very rude, unhelpful and generally unknowledgeable. It's a shame when professors think they are so much better than their students and peers.

A lot of WebCT. A ridiculous amount of biographical information on authors. Little to no engaging textual analysis. Talks the class away with her favourite students about things that have no bearing on the course at all. Unclear assignment guidelines. You end up paying for her disorganization with your grade.
Karyn is a very smart and engaging prof; however, she is very very picky about the mechanics of writing.. very picky..

Horrible. If you like getting a good mark without doing anything/learning anything, Karyn's class is for you. The best part is you are required to do High School style writing assessments for a third year English course. I started out thinking this would be my favourite course this semester and I ended up dreading going to each useless class.

Karyn is a supportive professor. 387 is the second class I have taken with her. Her lecture style is mostly  informative and relevant. She willingly gives help and feedback on paper topics and papers, which I found essential for my success in 354. She is  flexible and allows students to explore genres and theoretical issues that they find interesti

Karyn is a wonderful professor, her lectures are entertaining and informative. The ONLY thing that would keep me from taking her courses are this group of girls who appear in all of her courses. They are morons, they usually have no interest in the books but love chatting, bringing in personal anecdotes to excuse their idiocy

Disorganised: yes. Passion for subject: yes. Loves classroom discussion. Found her lectures 2B related & informative. Very insistent on correct grammar and citation, but is a 300-level class, so students should know how to write correctly anyway. Found her 2B helpful; have saved some of her handouts for future ref. I recommend her for this class!

I've had profs who are scattered yet entertaining, she is just scattered. At a certain point it affects your mark, because her expectations are not clear and she is full of excuses for her lousy teaching methods. If you don't have time to put together organized, interesting lectures, don't teach! Seems kind of unfriendly too.

Although she is a nice person, I found that she did not really give enought instruction for assignments. She is also VERY picky about grammar/bibliographies, and will dock tons of marks for tiny mistakes. However, she is a really nice person.

I don't mind her being finicky with English, but when her instructions are unclear, we get confused, and at the end, whose fault would it be?  After each explanation of the assignments, a group of students went to ask her to clarify her instructions as they were stated differently from what she said and what she put on paper.

I took various courses with Karyn and found them all to be interesting. She really does know her subject matter & is always willing to share what she knows with her students. Karyn may appear to obsess over grammar but in the post-secondary & professional "real" worlds she knows that to be taken seriously her students need to adhere to grammar.

Try to avoid taking a class with her. Overly picky about grammar, bibliographies and other minute details. Always stressed out and full of excuses as to why she wasn't prepared for class. Maybe she's a smart lady, but she shouldn't be teaching.

GREAT PROF!!! I entered the course thinking I hated sci fi and fantasy and suprisingly enough, thanks to her reading selections I have a whole new genre open to me for the summer.  She has a relaxed way about her and is VERY considerate of her students.  Go to class, work hard and you'll be fine with Karyn!

She is a kind woman, however, the class was MORE than disorganized.  She would often miss classes, show up late, and was NEVER prepared for lecture.  She was constantly revising the course outline on webct because she could never get the internet readings to us on time--sometimes she would post them the night before, othertimes the morning of.

Karyn is very intelligent, but her knowledge of the subject doesn't come through in her lecture. Seminars are long, pay attention to grammar, books aren't done justice by her lectures, takes attendance, lots of webct and exercises, critical reviews, etc. high maintenance prof and course. Feels like a book club - no intelligent discussion.

If you are not technically inclined, you won't like her. But then again, you won't be reading this, either. She likes her subject matter and expects you to read it, but appreciates discussion and is open to different opinions. Beware: Your term paper outline should be a paper by itself, not a one -page suggestion, and write your bibliography first.
She is a really nice person and very helpful...

She's a wonderful person who really is helpful if you make the effort to see her one on one. Very picky about Works Cited and Bibliographies. PAY ATTENTION to her guidelines. Overall very enjoyable experience. I would take a class with her again.
